free_text: calibration of fission fragments detectors via
U-235(n,f) reaction and using 241Am reference source.
Tl-180 was produced by 1.4 GeV proton induced
spallation reactions of a thick UCx (50 g/cm2) target
at ISOLDE High Resolution Separator as high-purity
beam ( 30-keV Tl+ ions, ~150 atoms/sec), which was
implanted in a 20 ug/cm2 thick carbon foil.
.1 Ortec Surface Barrier detector of active area
450 mm2, thickness 300 um, with a central hole of 6 mm
diameter, placed at ~7 mm distance upstream of the
.2 Canberra PIPS detector 300 mm2 active area, 300 um
thickness, placed at ~4 mm downstream of the foil.
. The total detection efficiency for alpha-particle or
single fission fragment ~ 51.% for any detector.
about 16%.  Measured energy resolution (FWHM) for alpha
decays in the 5. - 7. MeV range was ~35 keV.
Energy range of detection 200 keV - 100 MeV.

Three HPGe crystals and planar Ge detector of
"low-energy Ge" type were paced outside the vacuum
chamber to measure gamma and K x-rays in coincidence
with alpha and beta particles and fission fragments.
